pass1_value.note: Developers can create a Partner account (free) and create apps and development stores to obtain API credentials via the Partner Dashboard.

final_value.note: Shopify's official 'Create a Shopify app' guide states you need a Partner account to create an app (via Partner Dashboard or Shopify CLI). Creating an app in the Partner Dashboard provides app credentials (API key/secret) for API access, and Partner accounts/dev tooling are self-serve.

pass1_value.note: With a free Partner account and development store, developers can obtain API credentials and use REST/GraphQL APIs immediately. Some advanced features (e.g., Multipass, certain checkout extensibility) require Shopify Plus or additional feature access which may restrict parts of integration.

final_value.note: Shopify's official 'Getting started' / 'Create a Shopify app' docs indicate you can self-serve by creating a Partner account and creating an app (and using a development store for testing). The Shopify APIs documentation provides immediate access to API docs (Admin/Storefront), enabling building and testing integrations today; some advanced features may still require additional Shopify plan/feature access.
